authoritative_sources.title: Transmission Line Vegetation Management
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FERC is the primary federal regulator for bulk-power reliability oversight and explains how transmission vegetation obligations relate to the broader utility reliability regime.
used_for: separating transmission reliability scope from generic distribution work-order assumptions
defining why the lane cannot claim regulator or final authority

authoritative_sources.title: FAC-003-5 Transmission Vegetation Management
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FAC-003-5 is the primary reliability standard naming annual work plans, inspections, corrective actions, and evidence forms for transmission vegetation management.
used_for: terminology such as MVCD and ROW

authoritative_sources.title: 29 CFR 1910.269 - Electric power generation, transmission, and distribution
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: OSHA provides the controlling federal worker-safety baseline for electric utility vegetation work, including line-clearance tree trimming distance and staffing constraints.
used_for: refusal triggers for energized-work approval requests

authoritative_sources.title: 2026-28 Wildfire Mitigation Plan Guidelines
authoritative_sources.publisher: California Office of Energy Infrastructure Safety
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Energy Safety is a primary state utility safety regulator and its wildfire mitigation plan guidance is a public, lane-specific workflow reference for vegetation commitments and risk-prioritization documentation.

authoritative_sources.title: Environmental Science Division
authoritative_sources.publisher: California Office of Energy Infrastructure Safety
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Energy Safety's Environmental Science Division describes the regulator's audit, field inspection, and notice-of-violation functions for utility vegetation management.
used_for: failure-handling posture when commitments are not met

authoritative_sources.title: Energy Safety Releases Liberty Utilities' 2024 Vegetation Management Audit Report
authoritative_sources.publisher: California Office of Energy Infrastructure Safety
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The audit release gives concrete examples of regulator-accepted vegetation evidence categories, including QA/QC and open work-order documentation.

authoritative_sources.title: Powerline Administration and Management
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The Forest Service is the authoritative federal land manager for National Forest System lands and names permit, operating-plan, and annual-report requirements that change vegetation work sequencing on federal land.

workflow_stages.name: Demand intake and prerequisite validation
workflow_stages.description: Normalize patrol findings, circuit scope, asset class, and annual-plan demand into schedulable work only when required records are present.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Constraint and capacity assessment
workflow_stages.description: Assess annual work-plan obligations, crew and outage capacity, permit/access blockers, and wildfire or reliability thresholds before allocation decisions.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Schedule and allocation recommendation
workflow_stages.description: Recommend sequence, deferral, and contractor allocation while preserving circuit risk context, service levels, and documented tie-break rules.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Permit, access, and regulated-threshold escalation
workflow_stages.description: Escalate when landowner, permit, wildfire, or safety boundaries prevent work or materially change the valid recommendation.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Boundary and approval enforcement
workflow_stages.description: Keep the lane recommendation-only and hand off requests requiring switching, legal, environmental, or formal approval authority.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

artifacts: permit, operating-plan, easement, and landowner constraint records
contractor roster and outage window calendars
QA/QC and open work-order backlog records
systems_of_record: inspection, patrol, or LiDAR evidence repository
wildfire mitigation plan filing and audit evidence store
permit, easement, and land-access record system
crew, contractor, and outage scheduling systems

allowed: Recommend schedules, sequencing, and contractor allocation within documented utility vegetation constraints.
Validate the minimum public and tenant records required before planning proceeds.
Record tradeoffs, blocker causes, and next-owner escalation when access, safety, or approvals are unresolved.
escalate: Conflicting regulator, asset-class, or land-manager rules that change which schedule is valid.
Requests dependent on switching authority, qualified safety review, environmental or herbicide approval, or legal/easement resolution.
Capacity gaps or backlog tradeoffs that require approval beyond the lane.
refuse: Final approval requests for field release or regulator-complete attestation.
Requests to authorize energized line-clearance work or waive safety distances.
Requests that treat the industry label as cosmetic and omit vegetation-specific records.

domain_failure_modes: generic planning output that ignores MVCD, wildfire zone, ROW, or permit/access evidence
schedule recommendations made without patrol, LiDAR, or asset-class records that materially affect priority
failure to separate transmission reliability obligations from distribution wildfire-program obligations
overreach into switching, herbicide, environmental, legal, or regulator-facing approval authority

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Transmission reliability rules versus distribution wildfire programs
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: FAC-003 governs applicable transmission lines, while state wildfire or local distribution programs may govern lower-voltage circuits; runtime must know asset class before schedule advice is high confidence.
Herbicide licensing, environmental windows, and land-manager conditions differ by jurisdiction and tenant operating model, so they must be retrieved rather than guessed.

notes: The lane is materially different from generic planning because utility vegetation work is driven by right-of-way constraints, patrol evidence, wildfire commitments, and safety/permit boundaries.
validated quality is achievable without blocking ambiguity because jurisdiction-specific differences are explicitly converted into runtime prerequisites and escalation paths.
